description_long: This object consists of two vertical stacks of roughly hewn, rectangular stone blocks, each set on a square stone base. The stones are irregular in shape and size, with visible chips, cracks, and weathered surfaces, giving them an ancient, timeworn appearance. Scattered around the bases are several loose stones and fragments, suggesting a partially ruined or deteriorated structure. The coloration is predominantly gray with patches of mossy green and brown, indicating exposure to the elements. The surface texture is coarse and uneven, with clear signs of erosion and natural wear. The overall arrangement suggests a barrier or obstacle, possibly used to demarcate space or restrict passage in an urban or park setting.
description: Two weathered stone pillars with chipped, irregular blocks and scattered loose stones, showing signs of age and exposure. The surfaces are rough, gray, and mottled with mossy patches.
description_view_0: Front view showing both stone pillars standing upright, with several loose stones scattered around the bases.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the uneven stacking and slight lean of the left pillar, with more visible gaps between the stones.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the irregular alignment of the blocks and the distribution of loose stones behind the pillars.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the pronounced curve of the left pillar and the arrangement of loose stones to the right.
attribute_placement: stacked with other stones, set as a freestanding barrier or marker
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and natural veining, with some patches of moss and lichen; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and eroded with visible chips, cracks, and moss growth, indicating long-term exposure to weather.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with stone-paved or landscaped environments, partially surrounded by loose stones and possibly low vegetation.
